title: ILSpy disappears during processing without a trace
author: Csimbi
body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Download Pillars of Eternity II from Steam.
2. Start ILSpy.
3. Open <STEAM>\SteamApps\common\Pillars of Eternity II\PillarsOfEternityII_Data\Managed\Assembly-CSharp.dll
4. Hit CTRL+S. select save location, save and wait.
5. Observe ILSpy starts extraction but eventually disappears without a trace (about 75%?).

### Error message shown
N/A, there are no error messages.

### Details
ILSpy version 10.1.0.8386+98ee6c3b8410e495b2fbbe9ca414090a962202c8
.NET version 10.0.9-servicing.26270.113+901ca941248413c79832d2fdbd709da0c4386353
Selected C# and C# 13.0 / VS 2022.12 on top.

In contrast, when selecting C# and C# 15.0 / VS 202x.yy, it works correctly.

title: Negative length can make switch on string with dictionary transformation throw an exception
author: ds5678

title: Crash when clearing assembly list using delete key
author: dosvjazi
body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Open any project in ILSpy through context menu in VS solution explorer.
2. Open any class so dependencies will load.
3. Select all assemblies then delete them using "Delete" key.

### Error message shown

Error message is not shown, the app just crashing.

### Details
* Product in use: VS extension
* Version in use: 10.1.0.8386+98ee6c3b8410e495b2fbbe9ca414090a962202c8
* Seems like clearing assembly list through "File > Clear assembly list" does not crash the app.

title: No .sln File Produced and Dependencies Were Not Installed
author: sohlae
body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Executed the `ilspycmd Books.API.dll --project --outputdir "C:\source\decompiled"` command
2. Opened the decomplied directory. No .sln file exists
3. Opened the project using the .csproj file. 

### Error message shown

```
The type or namespace name 'AutoMapper' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
```

### Details
* Product in use: e.g. ILSpy dotnet tool for Windows
* Version in use: 10.1.0.8386
